title: quercus garryana var. semota
intro: Quercus garryana var. semota, also known as Oregon white oak or Garry oak, is a variety of the white oak tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties due to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus garryana var. semota include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
